step_by_step_thinking:
Among the given options A through D, only option C "Dideoxyribonucleotides" does not fit into the components typically involved in performing a PCR assay. While DNA polymerase and Taq polymerase are crucial for synthesizing new strands by adding nucleotides complementary to the template strand, dideoxynucleotides are actually added to terminate elongated chains during sequencing reactions rather than being part of the amplification step in PCR where regular deoxynucleotides are utilized.
\boxed{C} Based on the evidence provided about PCR, we understand that while DNA polymerase (option A), Taq polymerase (option B), and DNA templates (option D) are all essential components required for carrying out a PCR reaction, dideoxyribonucleotides (ddNTPs) serve a different purpose—they are used in Sanger sequencing to generate sequence reads, not in the amplification steps of PCR.
